mechanism_narrative: A 5-month growth acceleration in real GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of accelerating real GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-month decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of declining industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of rising federal funds rates above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals a labor market shock that compresses consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of rising unemployment rates above 7.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month growth acceleration in real GDP and a 1-month rise in housing starts signal a joint expansion in aggregate demand and housing market activity that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of joint growth acceleration in real GDP and housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signal a joint supply-side shock and labor market contraction that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of joint supply-side shock and labor market contraction.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 2-month decline in real GDP signal a joint monetary tightening cycle and aggregate demand shock that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of joint monetary tightening cycle and aggregate demand shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% and a 3-month decline in industrial production signal a joint labor market contraction and supply-side shock that compresses consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) in periods of joint labor market contraction and supply-side shock.
